paper_title: Microsatellite and Model-based Analysis of Betula Species

methods.description: Analysis of microsatellite data using PCO and Bayesian clustering to identify genetic clusters and admixture proportions among Betula species.

steps.description: Principal coordinates (PCO) analysis of microsatellite data was performed using POLYSAT implemented in R 2.15.3, based on pairwise genetic distance calculated by Bruvo's methods.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCO analysis using POLYSAT in R with Bruvo's genetic distance

steps.description: POLYSAT was used to transform the multilocus allele phenotype for each individual into binary arrays of the presence or absence of each allele.
steps.searchable_summary: transform multilocus allele phenotype into binary arrays using POLYSAT

steps.description: Further PCO analysis was performed using PAST 1.7.5 using pairwise Euclidean distances.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCO analysis using PAST with Euclidean distances

steps.description: Microsatellite data was analyzed with a Bayesian clustering approach in STRUCTURE 2.3.4 to identify the most likely number of genetic clusters (K).
steps.searchable_summary: perform Bayesian clustering using STRUCTURE to identify genetic clusters

steps.description: Performed ten replicates at each value of K from one to five under the admixture model with the assumption of correlated allele frequencies among populations.
steps.searchable_summary: run STRUCTURE replicates for different K values under admixture model

steps.description: The DK was calculated based on the rate of change in the log probability of the data between successive K values.
Replicate runs were grouped based on a symmetric similarity coefficient of >0.9 using the Greedy algorithm in CLUMPP.
steps.searchable_summary: group STRUCTURE replicates using CLUMPP with Greedy algorithm

steps.description: The slopes of the latitudinal clines in the admixture proportions were estimated using a mixed effects model, with slope as a fixed effect and population modeled as a random effect.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate slopes of latitudinal clines using mixed effects model

steps.description: The null distribution of slopes was estimated by permuting the latitudes among populations and repeating the analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate null distribution of slopes by permutation

steps.description: Pairwise F ST tests based on allele frequency were conducted for these populations in POLYSAT.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct pairwise F ST tests using POLYSAT

steps.description: A matrix of geographical distance was generated based on latitude and longitude in R package 'fields'.
steps.searchable_summary: generate geographical distance matrix using 'fields' package

steps.description: A Mantel test with 9999 permutations was conducted in R package 'ade4' to test for a significant signal of isolation by distance.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct Mantel test for isolation by distance using 'ade4'

methods.method_name: Model-based Prediction of Past Distribution Ranges
methods.description: Ecological niche models constructed with MAXENT to predict past distribution ranges of Betula species based on species occurrence records and environmental predictors.

steps.description: Ecological niche models constructed with MAXENT from species occurrence records.
steps.searchable_summary: construct ecological niche models using MAXENT

steps.description: Analysis of pairwise niche overlap revealed considerable similarity between B. pubescens and B. pendula niches.
Range overlap analysis at a conservative occurrence probability threshold identified extensive overlap between B. pubescens and B. pendula.
steps.searchable_summary: perform range overlap analysis with occurrence probability threshold
